ogTitle: Lake Clifton vs Lake Darlot: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lake Clifton and Lake Darlot.
heading: Lake Clifton vs Lake Darlot — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lake Clifton, Western Australia and Lake Darlot, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lake Clifton has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Lake Darlot (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lake Clifton or Lake Darlot safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lake Clifton has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Lake Darlot scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lake Clifton scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Lake Darlot scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lake Clifton or Lake Darlot?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lake Clifton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
